ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken nugget class (FOODON_03315595); no specific entry for egg mayo variant or set meal format. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers chicken nuggets generically; egg mayo sauce and set meal components are not separately coded. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for egg mayo chicken nuggets or this set meal format.

serving.description: 1 set meal (5 nuggets with egg mayo sauce, side, drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal portion of 5 egg mayo chicken nuggets with accompanying side and drink, weighing approximately 650 g total.

nutrient_source.title: Singapore Food Composition Database — Egg Mayo 5Pc Nuggets (Record G00010426)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 55.0 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g per 100 g) sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database record G00010426 at confidence 0.95. Secondary micronutrients (sugar 2.31 g, sodium 276.92 mg, dietary fibre 0.92 g, calcium 15.38 mg, iron 0.46 mg, cholesterol 38.46 mg, saturated fat 1.85 g, trans fat 0.08 g, vitamin C 2.31 mg) are also drawn from the HPB record as reported in the input; these carry a lower confidence of 0.70 as noted in the source metadata. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and remaining micronutrients not reported in the HPB record are set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 (Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J) — analogous estimate based on breaded fried chicken (GI ~45)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for egg mayo chicken nuggets. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on breaded fried chicken entries catalogued in the University of Sydney GI Database and published in Atkinson FS et al., 'International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021', American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021. The egg mayo sauce contributes negligible additional carbohydrate. The low carbohydrate content of the core item (2.36 g/100g) further supports a low GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (45) × carbohydrate per serving in grams (650 g × 2.36/100 = 15.34 g) / 100 = 6.9 (rounded to one decimal place).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 276.9 mg/100g; full set meal total sodium substantially higher due to potato fries and sugar-sweetened drink components
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this item sparingly, request reduced mayonnaise, avoid the accompanying potato fries and sugar-sweetened drink, and substitute water to limit total sodium intake and fluid-retention risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Core nugget item has low carbohydrate (2.36 g/100g) and high protein (10.7 g/100g), but the set meal includes high-GI potato fries and a sugar-sweetened beverage that significantly raise the overall glycaemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should order the nuggets alone without the potato fries and sugar-sweetened drink, substituting water or an unsweetened beverage and a salad side to maintain glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation, saturated fat (1.85 g/100g), trans fat (0.08 g/100g), cholesterol (38.46 mg/100g), and elevated sodium in the full set meal collectively raise cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, avoid the deep-fried potato fries component, request sauce on the side to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake, and choose water over sugar-sweetened beverages.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote joint inflammation; set meal also includes deep-fried fries and a refined-sugar beverage
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried chicken nuggets and request grilled chicken alternatives, omit potato fries, and replace the sugar-sweetened drink with water to minimise pro-inflammatory dietary load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Egg Mayo Chicken Nuggets (5-Piece Set Meal) is a Western-style fast food dish popular in Singapore, consisting of five deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets coated or served with egg mayonnaise sauce, typically sold as a set meal with potato fries and a soft drink. The nuggets are made from processed chicken meat encased in a seasoned breadcrumb coating and deep-fried until golden and crispy, then dressed with a creamy egg-based mayonnaise. The texture is crispy on the outside and tender inside, with a rich, savoury, mildly tangy flavour from the egg mayo sauce. Per 100 g of the core nugget item, the nutritional profile shows a high protein content of 10.7 g, very low carbohydrate at 2.36 g, and a reported fat value of 0.0 g from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (see notes for zero-fat justification), with sodium at 276.9 mg and cholesterol at 38.5 mg. The full set meal at 650 g significantly increases total energy, sodium, and carbohydrate intake due to the fries and beverage components. Clinically, the core nugget item has a low estimated glycaemic index (approximately 45) and low carbohydrate load, making it relatively suitable for glycaemic management when consumed without the high-GI accompaniments. However, the deep-fried preparation method generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), and the sodium content warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. This dish is commonly consumed at lunch or dinner in Singapore fast food outlets and hawker-adjacent settings.

How many calories in egg mayo nuggets?

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database record G00010426 at confidence 0.95. Secondary micronutrients carry a lower confidence of 0.70 as noted in the source metadata. The reported fat value of 0.0 g/100g is anomalous for a deep-fried breaded product and is retained as reported from HPB but flagged. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, not a direct measurement. Overall confidence is medium.
notes: fat_g is reported as 0.0 g/100g by the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (record G00010426) for this item. This is anomalous for a deep-fried breaded chicken product and likely reflects the nutritional profile of the core egg-and-chicken component as recorded in the source database rather than the full fried preparation. The value is retained as confirmed zero per the HPB source but flagged for review; actual fat content of the fried nugget is expected to be higher. Zero-macro justification: fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Food Composition Database record G00010426 — this is flagged as potentially anomalous for a deep-fried product and should be verified against the original HPB laboratory data. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, B-vitamin panel, fat-soluble vitamins, and moisture data for this specific egg mayo chicken nugget preparation are not available in HPB Singapore Food Composition Database record G00010426 as of 2026. The set meal portion (650 g) includes sandwich/wrap, 5 nuggets, a side (potato fries), and a drink; per-100g values reflect the composite set meal as reported. GI is an analogous estimate based on breaded fried chicken from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2021); no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found. Halal status assumed based on Singapore fast food context; not independently verified.
